hypothesis: If a computational workflow integrating thermodynamic databases, cellular automata simulation, machine learning descriptors, and commercial supplier databases is applied to inorganic material synthesis planning, then it will identify cost-effective and thermodynamically feasible synthesis recipes for target materials such as Ca2SiO4 more efficiently than conventional trial-and-error experimental approaches, because combining thermodynamic optimization with real-time commercial availability and pricing constraints systematically narrows the synthesis design space to economically viable candidates.
